heading: Pretrial conference
label: 949p–2 Pretrial conference
notes: References in TextThe Classified Information Procedures Act, referred to in subsec. (b), is , , , which is set out in the Appendix to Title 18, Crimes and Criminal Procedure.Pub. L. 96–45694 Stat. 2025Oct. 15, 1980
amendments: References in TextThe Classified Information Procedures Act, referred to in subsec. (b), is , , , which is set out in the Appendix to Title 18, Crimes and Criminal Procedure.Pub. L. 96–45694 Stat. 2025Oct. 15, 1980

children.heading: .—Motion
children.label: a .—Motion
children.text: At any time after service of charges, any party may move for a pretrial conference to consider matters relating to classified information that may arise in connection with the prosecution.

children.heading: .—Conference
children.label: b .—Conference
children.text: Following a motion under subsection (a), or sua sponte, the military judge shall promptly hold a pretrial conference. Upon request by either party, the court shall hold such conference ex parte to the extent necessary to protect classified information from disclosure, in accordance with the practice of the Federal courts under the Classified Information Procedures Act (18 U.S.C. App.).

children.heading: Matters To Be Established at Pretrial Conference.—
children.label: c Matters To Be Established at Pretrial Conference.—
B
children.text: the provision of notice required by ; andsection 949p–5 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: the initiation of the procedure established by .section 949p–6 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: which relates to classified information; or

children.label: B
children.text: which may promote a fair and expeditious trial.

children.heading: .—Effect of Admissions by Accused at Pretrial Conference
children.label: d .—Effect of Admissions by Accused at Pretrial Conference
children.text: No admission made by the accused or by any counsel for the accused at a pretrial conference under this section may be used against the accused unless the admission is in writing and is signed by the accused and by the counsel for the accused.
